mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above trend signals a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.5 million signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the Consumer Price Index above 2% signals a monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in monetary tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below trend signals a supply-driven contraction that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply-driven contraction periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 6% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 1.2 million signals a demand-driven contraction that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in demand-driven contraction periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to outperform a simple buy-and-hold strategy over any 1-year rolling window.
